Language and Grammar

Language and Grammar

Writing standards, grammar rules, and microcopy patterns for Voyadores products. Version 1.0 — July 2025.

Brand voice & personality

Core brand attributes

Simple Clear, straightforward communication that eliminates confusion.
Modern Contemporary language that feels current and accessible.
Empowering Inspiring confidence and action in MSME owners.

Voice characteristics

Conversational yet professional Speak like a knowledgeable friend, not a corporate entity.
Confident but humble Assert expertise while staying approachable.
Solution-focused Always guide toward actionable outcomes.
Culturally aware Respectful of Filipino business culture and values.

Writing principles

1

Clarity first

  • Use active voice whenever possible
  • Keep sentences under 20 words
  • Choose familiar words over complex alternatives
  • Break up long paragraphs (max 3–4 sentences)
2

Empowerment through language

  • Use "you can" instead of "you should"
  • Focus on opportunities, not limitations
  • Celebrate small wins and progress
  • Frame challenges as growth opportunities
3

Cultural sensitivity

  • Use inclusive language that respects diverse Filipino communities
  • Acknowledge different business sizes and stages
  • Avoid assumptions about technical knowledge
  • Respect traditional business practices while introducing modern solutions

Content hierarchy

LevelPurposeExample
H1 — Primary headlineBold, benefit-driven statement"Grow your business with smart digital tools"
H2 — Secondary headlineSupporting details or features"Track sales, manage inventory, connect with customers"
H3 — Tertiary headlineSpecific features or sections"Real-time sales dashboard"
Lead paragraphHook readers with immediate valueOpens with the most important information
Body textProvide context and detailsSupports the lead with supporting information
CTA textClear, specific next steps"Generate your first report"

Addresses

Standard format for Philippine addresses.

Format [Unit/Floor], [Building Name] [Street Number] [Street Name] Brgy. [Barangay Name], [City/Municipality] [Province], [ZIP Code]
Do
Unit 301, Ortigas Center
123 Julia Vargas Avenue
Brgy. San Antonio, Pasig City
Metro Manila, 1605
Use complete and properly capitalized address elements
Don't
123 Julia Vargas Ave, Ortigas, Pasig, MM
Use abbreviations or lowercase barangay/city names

Capitalization

Sentence case

Default for UI, buttons, messages, navigation, and labels. Lowercase except first word and proper nouns.

Do
Add new employee
Generate monthly report
Password must be at least 8 characters
Don't
Add New Employee
GENERATE MONTHLY REPORT
PASSWORD MUST BE AT LEAST 8 CHARACTERS
Title case

Formal document titles, page titles, or major campaign headlines only.

Do
Employee Handbook and Policies
Annual Financial Report 2025
Sales Receipts
Don't
EMPLOYEE HANDBOOK AND POLICIES
annual financial report 2025
SALES RECEIPTs
ALL CAPS

Only for acronyms and emergency alerts.

Do
CEO, BIR, MSMEs, VAT
CRITICAL: Maintenance in 5 minutes
Don't
Ceo, Bir, msmes, Vat
Critical: Maintenance in 5 minutes
lowercase

Emails, domains, file names, and technical terms.

Do
support@voyadores.com
invoice_template.pdf
employee_id
Don't
SUPPORT@VOYADORES.COM
Invoice_Template.PDF
Employee_ID

Time & date

Time format

  • Use 12-hour format with AM/PM
  • Use seconds only when needed (e.g., logs)
  • Use "12:00 AM" / "12:00 PM" — not 00:00 / 24:00
Do
2:30 PM
9:00 AM
12:00 AM (midnight)
2:30:45 PM (logs only)
Don't
14:30
9:00am
00:00
2:30 PM (when seconds are required)

Date format

Use inFormatExample
System UIMM/DD/YYYY06/15/2025
Marketing / docsLong writtenJune 15, 2025 or 15 June 2025
Logs (ISO format)ISO 86012025-06-15T14:30:00+08:00

Days & months

Full3-letter (UI only)Avoid
MondayMonM
JanuaryJan01

Time durations

UnitBody textUI
5 seconds"5 seconds left""5 s"
30 minutes"Complete this in 30 minutes""30 min"
2 hours"Expected wait: 2 hours""2 h"
3 days"We'll notify you in 3 days""3 d"
2 weeks"Trial period is 2 weeks""2 wk"
6 months"Contract lasts 6 months""6 mo"

Time & date ranges

Use an en dash (–) with no spaces.

Range typeFormatExample
Time9:00 AM–5:00 PMUse in schedules
Date (same month)June 15–20, 2025Billing period
Date (different month)June 15–July 5, 2025Leave approval
Date (different year)Dec 20, 2024–Jan 2, 2025Holiday closure
Timestamp2025-06-15T14:30:00ZFor logs

Currency & numbers

Currency

  • Use ₱ before the amount — not "PHP"
  • Always include two decimal places when needed
  • Use "to" for currency ranges (not a hyphen)
Do
₱1,500
₱25.50
₱500 to ₱1,000
Don't
PHP 1500
₱25.5
₱500–₱1,000

Numbers & numerals

RuleExample
Spell out 1–9"three reports", "nine days"
Use numerals for 10+"15 users", "25 modules"
Always use numerals with units"3 kg", "5 minutes"
Spell out if starting a sentence"Twelve employees joined."
Use numerals in UI"Save 3 records", "Upload 12 files"

Measurements & units

Use metric units. Spell out in content; use abbreviations in UI or tables.

MeasurementBody textUI
Distance10 kilometers10 km
Weight5 kilograms5 kg
Storage128 gigabytes128 GB
Resolution1920 × 1080 pixels1920 px × 1080 px

Punctuation

Commas (,)

  • Oxford comma: Always use in series — "Sales, inventory, and customer management"
  • Thousands separator: ₱1,500
  • Dates: "June 15, 2025"
  • Compound sentences: "Save your work, and then log out"

Dashes

  • En dash (–): Ranges — "Pages 15–30", "9:00 AM–5:00 PM", "January–March 2025". No spaces.
  • Em dash (—): Parenthetical statements — "The results—our best yet—exceeded expectations". No spaces in formal writing.
  • Hyphen (-): Compound words ("Co-owner", "Real-time"), prefixes ("Pre-launch"), phone numbers ("0917-123-4567"). Never use for ranges.

Periods (.)

  • End complete sentences
  • Omit in headings and UI labels
  • Omit in bulleted lists with fragments
  • Use in abbreviations: "Inc.", "Ltd.", "Corp."
  • Decimal points: "₱1,250.50"

Exclamation marks (!)

  • Use sparingly — only for genuine enthusiasm
  • Good uses: Welcome messages, success notifications
  • Avoid: Instructions, error messages, formal content
  • Never use multiple: one is enough

Ellipses (...)

  • Indicate truncation in UI when text is cut off
  • Three dots with no spaces between
  • Space before and after in body text sentences
  • Avoid in formal content — use "and more" instead

Apostrophes (')

  • Possessives: "The business's growth" (singular); "Businesses' needs" (plural)
  • Contractions: Only in casual contexts
  • Avoid contractions in error messages, official content, or logs
  • Don't use for plurals — no "1990's"

Colons (:)

  • Introduce lists or explanations
  • Don't capitalize the word after a colon unless it's a proper noun
  • Use in time: "2:30 PM"
  • Ratios: "3:1 ratio"

Parentheses ( )

  • Acronym definitions: "MSMEs (Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises)"
  • Examples: "Popular accounting software (QuickBooks, Xero)"
  • Phone area codes: "(02) 8123-4567"
  • Don't overuse — limit to maintain readability

Slashes (/)

  • Alternatives: "Employee/contractor status"
  • Dates: Acceptable in technical contexts — "06/15/2025"
  • Avoid "and/or" — choose one or rephrase

Semicolons (;)

  • Generally avoid — use periods or break into separate sentences
  • Acceptable for complex series with internal commas
  • "Our offices are in Manila; Cebu City; and Singapore"

Special characters

SymbolUseExample
@ (at symbol)Email addresses and social handlessupport@voyadores.com · @VoyadoresOfficial
# (hash)Reference numbers and social media onlyInvoice #2025-001
% (percent)Always use symbol, no space, en dash for ranges15% · 10%–15%
° (degree)Temperature (no space) and angles25°C · 90° angle
| (vertical bar)Separating navigation, breadcrumbs, optionsHome | Products | Contact
_ (underscore)File names and database fields onlyemployee_handbook.pdf · employee_id
* (asterisk)Required form fields, footnotesFirst name *
& (ampersand)Acceptable in company names and space-constrained UI labels onlyAvoid in body text — use "and"

Formatting guidelines

Text emphasis

StyleUse forAvoid
BoldKey information, UI element names in instructions, warningsEntire sentences; overuse reduces impact
ItalicsEmphasis on specific words, first-time terms, foreign words, product namesLong passages
UnderlineHyperlinks onlyDecorative emphasis — confuses users with links
StrikethroughDeleted content, price comparisonsFormal content

Headings

  • H1: One per page — main topic
  • H2: Major sections; H3: Subsections; H4: Detailed breakdowns (use sparingly)
  • Use sentence case — not title case
  • No punctuation at the end of headings
  • No bold, italics, or other emphasis inside heading tags

Lists

  • Use bullet lists for unordered items with parallel structure
  • Use numbered lists for sequential steps or ranked items
  • No periods if items are sentence fragments; use periods for complete sentences
  • Maximum 7 items per list for readability

Links

Do
Learn about inventory management
Browse the help center
Read the full study on Philippine business trends (external site)
Don't
Click here
Read more
Visit our pricing page link

Code & technical text

  • Use monospace / inline code for code snippets, file names, and field names
  • Use code blocks for multi-line examples
  • Example: employee_data.csv, admin123

Truncation

  • Use ellipses (...) when text is cut off
  • Truncate at meaningful points — don't cut words in half
  • Always provide a way to see the full text (hover tooltip, expand, or separate page)
Headlines (SEO)60 characters
Meta descriptions150–160 characters
Social mediaPlatform-specific limits

Accessibility guidelines

Plain language

  • Write at 8th-grade reading level
  • Define technical terms when first used
  • Use familiar words over complex alternatives
  • Break up long paragraphs into shorter chunks
  • Active voice: "You can generate reports" vs "Reports can be generated"

Alt text for images

  • Describe purpose, not appearance: "Sales dashboard showing ₱50,000 revenue increase" not "Screenshot of graphs"
  • Charts: Include key insights and trends
  • Functional icons: Describe the action — "Edit employee details" not "Pencil icon"
  • Decorative images: Use empty alt text (alt="")

Writing for screen readers

  • Write complete sentences — avoid fragments
  • Spell out abbreviations on first use: "MSMEs (Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises)"
  • Avoid directional language: "In the section below" instead of "See below"
  • Error messages: Be specific about what went wrong and how to fix it

Color and visual cues

  • Never rely on color alone — always provide text alternatives
  • Status indicators: Use text, icons, or patterns alongside color
  • Links: Identifiable without color (underlines, icons)

Components microcopy

1 Table — long text

Ensure readability of long text across the application.

  • Truncate the text
  • Use a tooltip to show the full content
Example Your session has expired due to inac…

2 Form labels

Consistent styling and clarity for form labels.

  • Use sentence case
  • Keep labels short and descriptive
  • Use the required attribute for mandatory fields
Examples First name · Date of completion

3 Placeholders

  • Input field: Do not use a placeholder
  • Text area: Do not use a placeholder
  • Select field: Use sentence case — "Select a/an [form-label]" or "Filter by [form-label]"
Examples Select a customer · Filter by status

4 Error messages

Guide users to complete the form.

Required field [Form-label] is a required field
Examples Address is a required field
Context-based
Examples We need this to ensure delivery reaches the correct location.
Start time should be entered in 12-hour format.
Add at least one invoice item.

5 Info labels

Clarify, explain, or provide helpful guidance about a form field.

[Form-label] [purpose]
Examples Address is where we'll send your physical documents.
Pay type refers to how employees are compensated for their work.

6 Modal — confirmation

Header [Action verb] [subject]
Examples Delete employee · Approve payroll adjustment
Body <Confirmation question> <Consequence>
Examples Are you sure you want to delete this report? This will permanently remove it from your records.

7 Modal header

[Action verb] [subject]
Examples View employee EMP-00040
Edit invoice #INV-00155

8 Link text

Help users navigate or take secondary actions.

  • Use sentence case
  • Be descriptive — avoid "click here"
  • Use concise, scannable phrases
Examples Read the full article
Learn more about Payroll Generation
Browse help center

Button labels

Button text rules

  • Use sentence case
  • Start with an action verb
  • Be specific and concise
  • Desktop: [Action verb] [subject (optional)] — Create, Update, Approve payroll
  • Mobile action button: [Action verb] — Create, Update, Approve
Label Definition Icon Confirms?
Accept Confirms a prompt, setting, or request—often implying agreement or consent. vi-check Yes
Activate Enables a feature or status. Counterpart of Deactivate. vi-power-off Yes
Add Inserts an item into an existing set or structure. Lightweight and immediate. vi-plus No
Apply Commits selected changes without leaving a form. vi-star-magic Yes
Approve Grants permission to move a request forward in a workflow. vi-like Yes
Cancel Stops an in-progress action or closes a modal without saving. vi-ban No
Close Dismisses a screen or component without implying any action was taken. vi-times No
Create Initiates and finalizes a new, standalone record or object. vi-plus No
Deactivate Disables a feature without deleting it. Often reversible. vi-power-off Yes
Decline Explicitly rejects a pending request, ending its workflow. vi-dislike Yes
Delete Permanently removes an item. Use only for irreversible actions. vi-trash Yes
Dismiss Silently closes an element (alert, banner) without saving. No data changes. vi-times-circle No
Download Transfers files from the system to the user's device. vi-download No
Draft Saves an unfinished version for future completion. Not final. vi-pen-line No
Edit Opens an existing record to modify its content. vi-pen No
Export Downloads system data in another format (CSV, Excel, PDF). vi-upload No
Generate Produces output (documents, reports, schedules) from existing inputs. vi-bolt No
Import Uploads and integrates external data into the system. vi-download No
Preview Displays a non-editable version of content before confirming. vi-file-text No
Reject Denies a submission and may return the item for revision. vi-ban Yes
Remove Detaches an item from a list or relationship. Less final than Delete. vi-minus-circle Yes
Save Stores current progress without submitting for review. vi-floppy-disk No
Send Delivers a message or document to a recipient. Triggers a notification. vi-send Yes
Submit Finalizes and forwards a form to the next workflow step. vi-send-right No
Update Applies new or modified data to an existing item. vi-rotate No
Upload Transfers files from the user's device to the system. vi-upload-alt No
View Opens a record in read-only mode. No edits or actions expected. vi-eye No

Notifications, states & alerts

Success

Reassure users that their action was successful.

[Subject] has been successfully [action]. [Impact (optional)]
Examples Payroll has been successfully disbursed. Employees can now see their payslip.
Invoice has been successfully voided. The client will not be billed for this invoice.

Error

Communicate what went wrong and what the user can do about it.

  • Avoid blaming the user
  • Avoid technical jargon
  • Be specific about what failed
[Subject] [action] was unsuccessful. [Reason]. [Resolution]
Example Payroll generation was unsuccessful. Review employees' attendance and try generating again.

Empty state

Communicate that there is no content to show.

  • Use sentence case
  • Avoid technical jargon
  • Include helpful guidance
No [subjects] were found. [Resolution (optional)]
Examples No invoices were found. Create an invoice to start tracking your billing.
No expenses were found. Add an expense to start tracking your spending.

Error state

Communicate that a system action or data retrieval has failed.

  • Avoid blaming the user
  • Be specific about what failed
  • Include resolution guidance
[Problem]. [Resolution (optional)]
Examples You don't have permission to access this page.
Access denied. Contact your admin to update permissions.
This document should be reviewed and updated quarterly to ensure continued relevance and effectiveness for Voyadores' growing community. Version 1.0 — July 2025
On this page
